summary: Add `fab uninstall` command to @fenglimg/fabric-cli — symmetric inverse of `fab init`. Six tasks: extract shared destination-path constants (refactor, no behavior change); create the core uninstall orchestrator with scaffold + MCP stages and command registration; build bootstrap-stage uninstall helpers (skills + hooks + un-merge + pointer-strip); add cli.uninstall.* i18n keys in en + zh-CN; comprehensive unit + integration + cli-surface + minimal i18n snapshot tests; moderate docs (CHANGELOG rc.9 + test-seed §1 with I/T invariants + README quick-ref). Target release: v2.0.0-rc.9.
approach: Three-phase symmetric inversion of init's pipeline (scaffold → bootstrap → MCP) backed by a single source of truth for destination paths. Foundation (TASK-001) extracts hard-coded install destinations into exported const tables consumed by both install (refactored, behavior-preserving) and uninstall (new). Core (TASK-002) creates the citty defineCommand mirroring init's three-phase orchestrator (resolveIntent → buildPlan → executePlan), implementing scaffold removal and MCP unregistration; TASK-003 in parallel implements bootstrap-stage helpers (skill removers, hook-script removers, conservative un-merge with optional --clean-empties cascade, pointer-line stripping). Defaults: full inverse; preserve `.fabric/knowledge/` and state files unless --purge; NEVER touch `~/.fabric/knowledge/`. After core, TASK-004 adds parallel cli.uninstall.* i18n keys in both locales. TASK-005 mirrors the init test pattern (real-fs + tmpdir + cocos-stub round-trip) with unit + integration + cli-surface drift gate update + minimal i18n snapshot (description + usage only, per binding clarification). TASK-006 lands moderate docs in parallel with tests. Idempotent + best-effort everywhere: missing artifacts skip, never error.
estimated_time: 4-6 hours of focused work (TASK-001: 30m refactor; TASK-002: 60-90m core command; TASK-003: 60m helpers; TASK-004: 30m i18n; TASK-005: 60-90m tests; TASK-006: 30m docs)

design_decisions.decision: Single top-level `fab uninstall` command (not `fab init --uninstall` flag, not subcommand chain)
design_decisions.rationale: Discoverability symmetric with `fab init`; binding user clarification #4 specifies the flag surface; per-stage opt-out via --no-bootstrap/--no-mcp/--no-scaffold preserves flexibility without polluting init's args
design_decisions.tradeoff: New ~600-line command module duplicates orchestrator scaffolding from init.ts; mitigated by shared constants from TASK-001

design_decisions.decision: Exported destination-path constants in install/skills-and-hooks.ts (no runtime install-manifest.json this cycle)
design_decisions.rationale: rc.7/8 users have no manifest; constants give compile-time link between install + uninstall; user clarification #3 binds. Future: manifest can be added later without breaking constants contract
design_decisions.tradeoff: Constants must be updated when templates evolve; co-location with helpers keeps the diff local

design_decisions.decision: Conservative un-merge by default, --clean-empties opt-in cascade cleanup
design_decisions.rationale: Deep-merged hook configs (.claude/settings.json etc.) contain user-authored entries alongside fabric entries; rm'ing loses user data. Match by `command`-path against FABRIC_HOOK_COMMAND_PATHS is bulletproof. Clarification #5 binds
design_decisions.tradeoff: Default leaves empty arrays/objects in config files (cosmetic, not functional); --clean-empties available for users who want pristine state

design_decisions.decision: Preserve `.fabric/knowledge/` and state files (events.jsonl, agents.meta.json, forensic.json) unless --purge
design_decisions.rationale: These contain post-init user-authored content (knowledge entries, audit ledger). Destructive default would lose work silently. Clarification #1+#2 bind
design_decisions.tradeoff: Default uninstall is not fully symmetric with init (init creates these files; uninstall preserves them) — discoverability handled via README + --help text

design_decisions.rationale: Personal root is shared across all projects; removing it from one project's uninstall corrupts others. Clarification #1+#2 bind
design_decisions.tradeoff: No way to clean personal root via uninstall; users must rm manually (acceptable since cross-project state is rare)

design_decisions.decision: Minimal i18n snapshot for uninstall (description + help-text usage only)
design_decisions.rationale: Uninstall output copy will iterate across rc.9/10/etc.; capturing full stdout in snapshot would create churn. Clarification #8 binds
design_decisions.tradeoff: Less drift protection for uninstall stdout strings during the iteration period; init/scan/serve continue to capture full stdout

design_decisions.decision: Pointer-line strip preserves file even when becomes empty (cannot prove init created it)
design_decisions.rationale: Install never persisted AgentsMdAction='created' vs 'preserved'; uninstall cannot retroactively distinguish. Conservative default mirrors init's preserve-if-present rule. Clarification #9 binds
design_decisions.tradeoff: User who installed fabric on a fresh project and then uninstalled may have an empty AGENTS.md left behind; manual rm is trivial

design_decisions.decision: Co-locate uninstall bootstrap helpers in install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts (not inside skills-and-hooks.ts)
design_decisions.rationale: Source of truth (install paths) stays in skills-and-hooks.ts; inverse logic lives next door consuming the same constants. Keeps each file under readability threshold
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two files to keep in sync if templates evolve; mitigated by shared constants — destination changes only need a constant edit, helpers compile against the new value automatically

design_decisions.decision: Per-client MCP `remove(serverName)` added to ClientWriter abstraction (clarification #10)
design_decisions.rationale: Three config formats (JSON/JSON/TOML) require three different removal paths; encapsulating in the writer mirrors how install's write() works. Single uninstallMcpClients() loop calls writer.remove('fabric') across detected clients
design_decisions.tradeoff: ClientWriter interface grows; each writer must implement remove; trivial since each already implements read+write

dimensions.completeness: All binding user clarifications (#1-#10) reflected; exploration coverage: install-architecture → TASK-001/002/003, integration-points → TASK-002/005/006, testing → TASK-005
dimensions.granularity: 6 tasks; each 30-90 min; TASK-002 and TASK-003 are largest but cohesive (single feature focus each)
dimensions.dependencies: Acyclic: TASK-001 → {TASK-002, TASK-003} → TASK-004 → TASK-005; TASK-006 parallel with TASK-004/005 after TASK-002+003
dimensions.convergence_criteria: Every task has 3+ quantified criteria with file paths and observable assertions (existsSync, JSON equality, exit code, snapshot diff, coverage threshold)
dimensions.implementation_steps: Each task has 4-11 ordered implementation steps with concrete file refs and operations
dimensions.constraint_compliance: Respects protected-tokens.ts; matches Keep-a-Changelog format; coverage gate ≥70% addressed in TASK-005; cli-surface drift gate addressed in TASK-005
